summary: Three of us at the co-working space, plus one teammate on Zoom from California. We ordered takeout: Thai, wraps, salads. Terri kicked things off with an activity to get us going, then we got into ideas for the Replit buildathon. We talked through a few: an ethical terms-and-conditions app, a tool to help international students with visa stuff, an ethical rideshare service. The T&C app got the most traction. The idea is that it would track updated terms across App Store apps and let users flag clauses that look sketchy before accepting. We spent most of the session digging into that one.

Morning at Back 9. None of us had really played golf before. Leilani and her friend picked the games, and the rules changed each round so we mostly just figured it out as we went. Different people won different games, which made it feel more fun than competitive. We had nachos, cheese curds, and jalapeño poppers.

Lunch at Bosphorus Cafe. Terri shared news on a couple of fronts: she's heading to Greece soon to present her paper on the AGAIN mindset, and she's going to a CEO retreat in Chicago next week. The conversation drifted into food spots in Indy and Hawaii, then into scuba, snorkeling, surfing, and shark stories. Vaishnava and Terri got into Spartan races and triathlons. The eggplant salsa and baklava were the standouts.

blurbs.name: Terri
blurbs.text: I could see the sea floor in Hawaii. Turtles. One shark.

blurbs.name: Agrima
blurbs.text: Once held an octopus underwater. Wouldn't recommend.

blurbs.name: Anushka
blurbs.text: Dead lobster, malfunctioning helmet, the whole thing was a lot.

blurbs.name: Vaishnava
blurbs.text: My scuba dive video has more fish in post-production than I saw in real life.
